content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS with length NNN. Determine if it is possible to find two non-empty strings AAA and BBB which satisfy the following conditions:


A+B=SA + B = SA+B=S, where +++ denotes string concatenation
BBB is a substring of AAA
Note: BBB is a substring of AAA if BBB can be obtained from AAA by deleting several (possibly zero) characters from the beginning and several (possibly zero) characters from the end. For example, "ab" is a substring of "cabd", but "ad" is not.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^6
S consists only of lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (the length of the string). The second line contains the string S of length N consisting of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to split S into non‑empty strings A and B such that A+B=S and B is a substring of A, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we only need to check whether the last character of the string appears earlier. In the first case 'b' appears at position 3, so YES. In the second case 'k' never appears before the end, so NO. The remaining cases follow the same rule.

solution.approach: Let the last character of S be c = S[N-1]. If B is a suffix of S that appears inside the prefix A, then the occurrence of B inside A contains its last character c, therefore c must also appear somewhere in A (i.e., in S[0..N-2]). Conversely, if c appears in the prefix, we can choose B to be the one‑character suffix "c"; this B is non‑empty, A=S[0..N-2] is non‑empty, and B is clearly a substring of A. Hence the condition is equivalent to: does the last character occur earlier in the string? The algorithm scans the string once, remembering the last character, and checks whether it appears in the first N‑1 positions. Time complexity O(N) per test case, total O(sum N). Space complexity O(1).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) overall

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.read().split()
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=int(next(it))
        s=next(it)
        last=s[-1]
        out.append('YES' if last in s[:-1] else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    solve()
